refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use by ensuring (1) timely data acquisition from vehicles and sensors, (2) efficient data processing and model update, and (3) prompt dissemination of updated model to relevant stakeholders.
refined_fr.rationale: To meet the strict latency requirement (p95 ≤ 1.0 s), the overall worksite update function is decomposed into sub-functions addressing data acquisition, processing, and dissemination, enabling targeted design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency from data acquisition start to updated model availability; verify each sub-FR latency budget; perform stress tests under operational scenarios.
sub_frs: Acquire real-time telemetry data from vehicles and sensors within latency budget
Process and update the worksite model efficiently within latency budget
Disseminate updated worksite model to stakeholders within latency budget

candidate_dps: Implement high-throughput telemetry data pipeline with prioritized message handling
Use in-memory data processing and incremental model update algorithms
Leverage low-latency network protocols and multicast dissemination
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement high-throughput telemetry data pipeline with prioritized message handling

potential_sources: Data acquisition tightly coupled with processing module due to latency constraints
Dissemination depends on updated model availability
coupling_check.notes: Coupling is expected but manageable with clear interface definitions and modular design.

assumptions_risks: Telemetry data sources provide timely and reliable data
Network link supports required bandwidth and latency
Processing algorithms can meet latency targets under load
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider refining dissemination DP if latency targets are not met in integration testing.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system responsible for telemetry acquisition, processing, and dissemination of worksite model updates

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time telemetry data from Vehicle module

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Sends updated worksite model to Teleoperation and Safety Services modules

module_containment_scores.notes: Centralizes telemetry processing and model update, minimizing diffusion.
Receives updates but does not process model updates.
Consumes safety alerts, not involved in model update.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing the worksite update logic in NGHE Solution module reduces latency and diffusion risks, enabling better control over update timeliness.
containment_risks: Potential bottleneck if NGHE Solution module becomes overloaded
Interface latency with Vehicle telemetry and Network Link must be tightly controlled
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Monitor module load and consider offloading processing to submodules if latency targets are not met.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FRs are well decomposed and the selected DP logically addresses the latency requirement with clear assumptions.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion due to centralized NGHE Solution module
Interfaces are well defined and limited in number
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores favor NGHE Solution as the owner, supporting architectural clarity.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed end-to-end and at sub-FR levels using telemetry and network latency measurements.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of FRs to NGHE Solution module with well-defined interfaces to Vehicle and other modules.

recommended_revisions: Refine dissemination DP if integration latency tests show delays
Monitor interface performance and consider modularization within NGHE Solution if needed

decision.justification: Current level of decomposition and assignments meet requirements and architectural clarity; no immediate need to zigzag or revise.
next_level_sub_frs: Acquire real-time telemetry data from vehicles and sensors within latency budget
Process and update the worksite model efficiently within latency budget
Disseminate updated worksite model to stakeholders within latency budget

revise.why: No critical issues identified requiring revision at this level.

assumptions: Telemetry data sources are reliable and timely
open_risks: Potential processing bottlenecks under peak load
evidence_needed: Latency measurements from telemetry acquisition to model update
Load testing results for processing algorithms
